file_name: Reviewer Criteria for Existing Data Preregistrations.pdf

pages_preview.text_preview: Existing Data Preregistration Reviewer Criteria The lists below are intended to guide reviewers in evaluating Existing Data preregistrations for SCORE. You don’t need to fill out a copy of the checklist, but should use it as a guide to the types of information that should be present in a finished preregistration - remember that not all items will apply to all projects. For each question, the response should include a complete description (a statement of the research plan in enough detail for another group to faithfully implement), list of deviations (clearly stating what has added, omitted, or changed from the original study), and rationale (altogether, is there a strong justification of why decisions are consistent with a good-faith replication of the original claim). At the end of the list are the final questions you should evaluate when deciding whether a preregistration is complete. Study Information 1. Title (No evaluation is necessary.) 2. Authors and Affiliations (No evaluation is necessary.) 3. Description of study (No evaluation is necessary - this section is created for every project by the SCORE research scientists to identify the specific finding and statistical evidence which constitute the focal claim for the paper. Depending on the type of project (replication, robustness, etc) the H* statistical evidence may not be relevant. This section should not be altered by the RR group.

Study Information 4. Research questions Existing data projects will have a series of research questions that inform the hypotheses (which are detailed in the following section). Review Criteria: ● Description: Does the section provide enough detail to evaluate what research questions will be examined in this preregistration? ○ Are the research questions labeled RQ1, RQ2, etc., with the SCORE focal hypothesis as RQ*? ○ Are the listed questions specific and concise? ○ Are questions identified as directional or non-directional, and, if applicable, have the direction been stated? ● Deviations: Does the list of research questions indicate whether additional questions are taken from the original study or modified/added by the team? ● Rationale: Does the description provide a rationale for the inclusion of any additional research questions (i.e. that they are logical deductions/operationalizations of the selected SCORE claim). 5. Hypotheses Every preregistration has a single hypothesis called H*, which is the specific claim used to evaluate the individual SCORE project. In addition, labs may choose to add additional hypotheses that are closely related to H*, either from the original study or new to this project (i.e. to give an alternative analysis of the focal claim). Any additional hypotheses should be extremely related to H*. If additional hypotheses seem more like extensions or less related to H*, these should be put into their own, separate pre-registration. Review Criteria: ● Description: Does the list above provide enough detail to evaluate what confirmatory hypotheses this preregistration is designed to test? ○ Are the hypotheses labeled H1, H2, etc., with the SCORE focal hypothesis as H*?

○ Are the listed hypotheses specific, concise, clearly testable, and specified at the level of operationalized variables? ○ Are hypotheses identified as directional or non-directional, and, if applicable, have the direction of hypotheses been stated? (Example: “Customers’ mean choice satisfaction will be higher in the CvSS architecture condition than in the standard attribute-by-attribute architecture condition.” ● Deviations: Does the list of hypotheses/tests indicate whether additional hypotheses are taken from the original study or modified/added by the team? ● Rationale: Does the description provide a rationale for the inclusion of any additional hypotheses (i.e. that they are logical deductions/operationalizations of the selected SCORE claim). Study Information 6. Datasets used Review Criteria: Please check that datasets are named and briefly described. 7. Dataset availability (multiple choice) Review Criteria: Please check that one of the three responses is bolded and that this question was not accidentally skipped. 8. Data access Review Criteria: This section should be used to describe any restrictions to accessing the dataset. Please check that the RR team has mentioned any permissions required for them to access the data.

9. Data identifiers Review Criteria: Please double check that the provided identifier identifies the correct datasets. If there are links provided, please follow the links and check they work. If there is another unique identifier, please try to search for it in a web browser to ensure they are working and correct for the datasets identified in previous sections. 10. Access date Review Criteria: Please double check that the date is written in the correct format: Month (spelled out in English), numeric date (without any suffix e.g., “th”, “rd”), numeric year. Good examples: April 1 2021 June 24 2021 March 3 2021 Bad examples: 4/1/2021 1/4/2021 June 24th 2021 3rd Mar 2021 11. Data collection procedures Review Criteria: Check that this section describes, to the best ability of the RR group, how the data was originally collected. Is it a longitudinal dataset comprised of interview sessions? Large, nationally representative samples collected by phone interview? Also check to see if RR groups mention how representative the data are, or if there are any supplemental materials from the data that better explain this. For projects that use the original author’s data, this may not be as detailed if there is not additional documentation shared from the original author.

12. Codebook Review Criteria: Some existing datasets will have codebooks available. If this preregistration is using such a dataset, is the codebook provided? It could be linked directly, or if downloaded and stored on the OSF project associated with this RR project, that could be linked as well. For projects that use the original author’s data, a codebook may not be available. Please check to make sure the RR group provides details about materials available other than the data, if any. Variables 13. Manipulated variables Note to reviewers: It is completely appropriate for many existing data projects to not have any manipulated variables. Please review this section in the context of the specific project scope. Review Criteria: ● Description: Does the preregistration list all manipulated variables (if any) in the study? ○ Does the preregistration state that the response includes all variables which are manipulated during the study? (If the SCORE focal test is conducted in the context of a larger study, variables which will not affect the testing of claim being replicated may summarized, but should still be disclosed) ○ For each of these variables, does the preregistration describe how each condition will be manipulated? ○ Does the preregistration comment on the role of each manipulated variable in the focal analyses (e.g., independent variable, moderator, etc)? ○ Does the preregistration provide links to manipulated materials (e.g., copies of questionnaires or stimuli in each condition) stored in the OSF project? ● Deviations: Does the preregistration note any significant deviations in the manipulated variables, or state that there are no changes? ○ If the replication study includes any additional manipulated variables, or if a factor manipulated in the original study is not included here, is this disclosed? ○ Does the preregistration describe any changes in procedure, context, or instruments used for these manipulated variables (e.g., sound condition played over headphones instead of speakers)?

● Rationale: Does the preregistration provide a justification for why decisions regarding the manipulated variables are consistent with a good-faith replication/reproduction effort of the focal claim? 14. Measured variables Review Criteria: ● Description: Does the preregistration comment on each measured variable involved in the focal analysis? ○ Does the preregistration comment on the role of each measured variable in the focal analyses (e.g., inclusion criteria, dependent variables, control variables, etc)? ○ If the study will measure variables which will not be involved in the focal analysis, are these variables disclosed? ○ Does the preregistration provide links to measurement materials (e.g., copies of questionnaires) stored in the OSF project, if relevant? ● Deviations: If the replication includes extra measured variables or omits variables from the original study, is this described? ○ Does the preregistration describe any changes in procedure, context, or instruments used for these measured variables (e.g., extraversion measured with EPI vs MIES)? ● Rationale: Does the preregistration provide a justification for why decisions regarding the measured variables are consistent with a good-faith replication/reproduction effort of the focal claim? 15. Unit of analysis Review Criteria: ● Description: Does the preregistration describe in sufficient detail the unit of analysis for the focal hypothesis H* and any additional hypotheses? ○ Are the units of analysis defined clearly? ○ Does the preregistration include the expected sample size after inclusion/exclusion of particular units of analysis, if relevant?
